hero.title: A complete AI assistant in under 1 MB
hero.sub: A local AI assistant that ships as one standalone binary under 1 MB — no Node, no Python, no SDKs to install. Point it at OpenAI, OpenRouter, or any OpenAI-compatible server and chat from your terminal or Telegram while it remembers facts, edits files, and runs scheduled tasks — each capability behind an on/off switch in config.

primary.label: Release binary
primary.code:
# download the asset for your OS/CPU from
# github.com/nullclaw/nllclw/releases/latest
chmod +x nllclw
./nllclw --help

alts.label: Build from source (Zig 0.16.0)
alts.code:
git clone https://github.com/nullclaw/nllclw.git
cd nllclw
zig build --release=small
./zig-out/bin/nllclw --help

quickstart:
Run the setup wizard once, then talk to it.

```bash
./nllclw init              # setup wizard: provider, style, capabilities
./nllclw "what are you?"   # one-shot question
./nllclw                   # interactive terminal chat
./nllclw status            # quick health line
./nllclw doctor            # full diagnostics
```

features.title: Stdlib-only single binary
features.body: One file you download and run. No Node, no Python, no curl, no provider SDK — the default build uses only Zig's standard library, including its own HTTP and streaming code.

features.title: Any OpenAI-compatible provider
features.body: Works with OpenAI, OpenRouter, or any server that speaks the OpenAI chat API — including local ones like Ollama. Replies stream token by token as they arrive.

features.title: Capability-gated tool loop
features.body: The model can list, read, write, and edit files, set cron schedules, search the web once you configure a search provider (Brave, Tavily, and others), and save its own macro tools. Every capability sits behind a config switch — set NLLCLW_FILE_WRITE=off and file writes are gone — and rounds per answer and output size are both capped.

features.title: Two-layer local memory
features.body: Keeps the recent conversation plus facts you ask it to remember ("remember that this project uses Zig 0.16"), stored as plain files on your disk. Nothing is synced anywhere.

features.title: Six channels, one runtime
features.body: Ask a one-shot question from the command line, chat in the terminal, message it on Telegram, connect a browser UI over WebSocket, have its heartbeat mode pick up pending tasks from a markdown TODO file, or run it as a daemon executing scheduled tasks — the same assistant, config, and memory everywhere.

features.title: Shell compiled out by default
features.body: shell_exec exists only in an explicit -Dshell-tool=true build. The default binary cannot run shell commands at all.

note: Pre-1.0 with one release (v2026.6.1); binary size and test counts are the project's own snapshot, and config and CLI may change between releases.

plain.what: A command-line AI assistant that ships as a single standalone binary under 1 MB, written in Zig, with chat, local memory, file tools, scheduling, Telegram, and a WebSocket API built in.
plain.why: Most AI assistants require a Node or Python runtime and bury their logic in a plugin platform; nllclw fits the whole assistant — provider calls, streaming, tools, memory, channels — into about 21,000 lines of dependency-free Zig you can actually read.
plain.when: Reach for it when you want an AI helper on a machine where you won't install Node or Python — a bare server, a container, your laptop — or when you want a complete agent implementation small enough to read end to end and fork into your own.
